title: eperua rubiginosa var. rubiginosa
intro: Eperua rubiginosa var. rubiginosa, also known as the "Ironwood" or "Sangre de Drago" due to its reddish sap, is a medicinal plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its rich antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin problems. Its medicinal properties can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds, which have been shown to have potential health benefits such as reducing oxidative stress, inflammation, and improving cardiovascular health. In traditional medicine, the plant's bark, sap, and leaves are prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues.
